mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate (below 2%) keeps the discount rate applied to equity cash flows compressed, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains in an accommodative range without abrupt upward moves.
regime_hint: any regime with accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more relative to its 12-month trailing minimum (an early Sahm-rule-style trigger), it signals labor-market deterioration that precedes recessionary repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.3 as of 2017-07, near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the 0.3pp trigger fires, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS month-over-month increase) combined with a rising FEDFUNDS rate jointly signal an overheating labor market that raises the probability of accelerated monetary tightening, producing negative equity returns over the following month because rate-hike expectations outweigh the positive growth signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% while FEDFUNDS is rising month-over-month
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle amid strong labor demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show positive mean 20d forward return with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or interaction Rank IC does not exceed the sum of single-series Rank ICs over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where retail sales (RSAFS) growth remains positive but is decelerating month-over-month, consumer demand momentum still supports short-term equity gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets price continued but slowing consumption strength.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating but positive consumption growth
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following positive RSAFS prints are not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05) over rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive year-over-year growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as long as the expansion trend persists.
regime_hint: any regime with expanding industrial output
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO YoY growth and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative INDPRO growth periods do not show correspondingly lower returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 5% signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as markets anticipate broader demand softening.
regime_hint: any regime, but strongest in rate-sensitive housing slowdowns
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of trigger instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation runs persistently above the FEDFUNDS nominal rate, implying a negative real policy rate, financial conditions remain effectively accommodative despite nominal tightening rhetoric, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter until the real rate turns positive.
candidate_trigger: 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate exceeds FEDFUNDS level (negative real rate)
failure_condition: 60d forward returns during negative real-rate episodes are not significantly higher than positive real-rate episodes (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC underperforms either single-series proxy.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where UNRATE is near multi-year lows (below 4.5%) and PAYEMS month-over-month growth begins decelerating for two consecutive months, the labor market signals peak-cycle exhaustion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets front-run slowing momentum.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS MoM growth decelerates for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following the joint trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC for the interaction term is not greater than either individual proxy's Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.
